duration:

3-4 classes: 3.5 hours (includes time for lunch and self-guided teacher activity – cemetery or Victorian era games)
description: Learn how an ancient world shaped the local landscape and attracted both coal and sand miners to this area. Discover what life was like for immigrant miners and their thriving communities while exploring what pieces of evidence remain. This program takes place entirely above ground.

description: Dive into the rocking world of geology as we walk though time investigating layers of sedimentary rock left by an ancient sea! Discover different rock types as we search through waste rock piles and find out about fabulous fossils of the East Bay.

program_name: Uncovering Local History: Stories of Social Justice and the Port Chicago 50
learning_standards: HSS-11.7, HSS-11.10. (Open for 9-12th grades; best suited for 11th grade US History Classes for alignment with standards.)
duration:
2-3 classes: 3 hours                                      
(2 class min, 3 class max)
description: Thurgood Marshall Regional Park - Home of the Port Chicago 50 honors World War II stories of civil rights and social injustice that took place on this land. This program explores how these events led to the eventual desegregation of the U.S. military – thus shaping our region, state, and beyond. Students will learn about the Port Chicago Disaster, Port Chicago 50, and various key figures.
limited_availability: Available only on Tuesdays and Thursdays; Closed June-September for heat safety

program_name: Story of the Port Chicago 50 - Virtual
learning_standards: HSS-11.7, HSS-11.10. (Open for 9-12th grades; best suited for 11th grade US History Classes for alignment with standards.)
max_chaperones: Not applicable, 1 teacher needs to be present
description: In this virtual program, learn the story of the Port Chicago Disaster and Port Chicago 50 – a story of social injustice, resistance, and bravery that occurred during World War II in Contra Costa County and that led to the eventual desegregation of the U.S. military. 1 class at a time.

learning_standards: 3-LS4-3, 3-LS4-4, 2-LS4-1, MS-LS2-3, MS-LS2-4, MS-ESS3-3, MS-ESS3-4, HS-LS2-4, HS-LS2-2, HS-LS2-7, HS-LS4-6, HS-ESS3-1, HS-ESS3-3, HS-ESS3-6
duration:
1-2 classes: 1.5 hours                                  
3-4 classes: 3.5 hours (includes time for lunch and self-guided teacher activity)
description:
Hike through the townsite of Somersville to learn of the immigrants who carved a life from these hills. Then continue underground into the Coal Mine Experience, a replica 19th century coal mine, to discover how and why coal was critical to the young state of California.
*Adaptable for grades 3-5, 6-8, or 9-12 with associated standards

metadata.title: Coal Mining History Tour  | Black Diamond Mines Visitor Center | East Bay Regional Park District
